goal: Fix issues found during a colony seal ceremony audit — charter-write silent failures, blocked wisdom promotions, missing midden-ERROR_LOG bridge, and Sage analytics accuracy

phases.name: Fix charter-write silent failures
phases.description: Make _insert_section_entries failures visible so charter-write does not silently drop entries

tasks.description: Add return-value checks after _insert_section_entries calls in queen.sh and log errors on failure
Update json_ok at queen.sh line 1554 to include sections_failed count; use json_err when all sections fail
Add test verifying charter-write returns error when QUEEN.md is missing expected section headers

success_criteria: charter-write returns json_err when all section insertions fail
New test passes for missing section headers

phases.name: Unblock wisdom promotions
phases.description: Lower pattern:auto threshold from 2 to 1 in both threshold functions so observations can auto-promote

tasks.description: Change pattern:auto from 2 to 1 in both get_wisdom_threshold() and get_wisdom_thresholds_json() in aether-utils.sh
Add lockstep verification test that asserts both threshold functions return identical values for all wisdom types

success_criteria: Both threshold functions return 1 for pattern:auto

phases.name: Build midden-ERROR_LOG bridge
phases.description: Create bridge subcommand that reads errors.log and converts entries into structured midden.json entries

tasks.description: Add _midden_ingest_errors function to midden.sh that parses errors.log and creates midden entries with category=error_log
Register midden-ingest-errors subcommand in aether-utils.sh dispatch table
Add tests for bridge covering: no errors.log, empty errors.log, and errors.log with multiple entries

success_criteria: midden-ingest-errors returns json_ok with ingested:0 when errors.log missing
Converts errors.log lines into midden entries
errors.log moved to errors.log.ingested after ingestion

phases.name: Improve Sage velocity outlier detection
phases.description: Add per-week variance, standard deviation, and outlier detection to Sage velocity analysis

tasks.description: Update Sage velocity return schema to include weekly_commits array, std_deviation, and outlier_weeks fields
Update Sage Step 4 analysis workflow to compute weekly variance and z-scores alongside existing half-window comparison

success_criteria: Sage schema includes weekly_commits, std_deviation, outlier_weeks
Both .claude and .aether agent mirrors are identical
Existing schema fields preserved for backward compat

phases.name: Fix Sage churn metric and anti-pattern
phases.description: Add project-level churn_summary to Sage and fix pipe-to-while anti-pattern that loses variables

tasks.description: Fix pipe-to-while anti-pattern in Sage churn extraction to use process substitution
Add churn_summary object to Sage schema with total_files_changed, churn_gini_coefficient, and trend
Run npm run lint:sync to verify agent mirror parity after all Sage changes

phase_learnings.learning: Process substitution avoids subshell variable loss in bash while loops
TDD with parallel background agents speeds up foundation work
Parallel agents for content authoring is efficient with independent file sets

phase_learnings.phase_name: Move oracle infrastructure to distributable location

learnings.claim: Watcher independently catches state-path bugs (line 895 archive using SCRIPT_DIR instead of ORACLE_STATE_DIR) that builders miss during refactoring
learnings.evidence: Vigil-96 caught the bug, fix was applied and verified

learnings.claim: When moving bash scripts, separate infrastructure (distributable code) from state (per-instance data) using distinct directory variables (SCRIPT_DIR vs ORACLE_STATE_DIR)
learnings.evidence: Successfully separated oracle.sh (utils/oracle/) from state files (oracle/) with ORACLE_STATE_DIR variable

learnings.claim: Anchor .npmignore patterns with leading slash when directory name appears at multiple depths to prevent unintended exclusion
learnings.evidence: Unanchored oracle/ would have excluded utils/oracle/ too; /oracle/ correctly scopes to top-level only

phase_learnings.phase_name: Update all oracle.sh path references across codebase

learnings.claim: SCRIPT_DIR references in test harness setup are easily missed when the main ORACLE_SH path constant is updated — grep for SCRIPT_DIR separately
learnings.evidence: Watcher caught 7 SCRIPT_DIR refs in oracle-colony tests that builders missed despite updating ORACLE_SH

learnings.claim: Distribution pipeline has three independent exclusion layers (.npmignore, HUB_EXCLUDE_DIRS, UPDATE_PROTECTED_AETHER_DIRS) — verify each separately
learnings.evidence: All three layers verified independently: .npmignore excludes oracle state, HUB_EXCLUDE_DIRS allows utils/oracle, UPDATE_PROTECTED_AETHER_DIRS protects oracle state

learnings.claim: Position-aware path exclusion (first-segment-only) is needed when a directory name appears at multiple depths in the tree
learnings.evidence: HUB_EXCLUDE_DIRS uses parts.some() which matches any segment — adding oracle would also block utils/oracle/. Fixed with HUB_EXCLUDE_FIRST_SEGMENT using parts[0] check.

learnings.claim: jq reset template creates fields that do not exist in input — useful for migration but can hide schema drift
learnings.evidence: Chaos ant found that applying reset to state without colony_version creates it as 0

learnings.claim: When writing jq output back to the same file, always guard against empty output — echo "" > file destroys the file
learnings.evidence: Chaos ant found that jq failure produces empty $updated variable, which when redirected destroys COLONY_STATE.json

instincts.action: Use process substitution not pipes for accumulation loops

instincts.trigger: moving bash scripts to new directory
instincts.action: Separate infrastructure variables (SCRIPT_DIR) from state variables (STATE_DIR) and grep for all SCRIPT_DIR references to catch missed state file paths
evidence: Watcher caught line 895 archive operation using SCRIPT_DIR instead of ORACLE_STATE_DIR

instincts.trigger: researching: What are the main components and their responsibilities in the Aether system?
instincts.action: Oracle found (85% confidence): System has 13 major components: aether-utils.sh (11,272-line monolithic bash utility with ~125 subcommands), CLI (Node.js bin/cli.js with 16 lib modules), 43 slash commands, 22 agent definitions acros
evidence: Oracle research: What are the main components and their responsibilities in the Aether system?

instincts.trigger: researching: What are the dependency relationships between components, and where are coupling risks?
instincts.action: Oracle found (82% confidence): COLONY_STATE.json is the central coupling nexus: 219 direct references across 38 of 43 slash commands, plus 153 references in aether-utils.sh. Both slash commands and aether-utils.sh read/write it —
evidence: Oracle research: What are the dependency relationships between components, and where are coupling risks?

instincts.trigger: researching: Where are the risk areas — execution reliability gaps, silent failures, fake autonomy, hallucination vectors, and verification weaknesses?
instincts.action: Oracle found (82% confidence): Silent error suppression at industrial scale: ~338 instances of error-swallowing patterns. ERR trap disabled for suggest-analyze (lines 10236-10427), creating ~200-line untrapped window during builds.
evidence: Oracle research: Where are the risk areas — execution reliability gaps, silent failures, fake autonomy, hallucination vectors, and verification weaknesses?

instincts.trigger: researching: How does the memory and context management system handle state persistence, session resume, context rot, and cross-colony knowledge sharing?
instincts.action: Oracle found (82% confidence): Dual-file state persistence: session.json (lightweight metadata) and COLONY_STATE.json (authoritative state). Resume reads both, COLONY_STATE.json always authoritative. Missing/corrupted COLONY_STATE.
evidence: Oracle research: How does the memory and context management system handle state persistence, session resume, context rot, and cross-colony knowledge sharing?

instincts.trigger: researching: What would an expert change about this architecture to minimize user input while maximizing safety, verifiability, and autonomous execution capability?
instincts.action: Oracle found (80% confidence): RECOMMENDATION 1 — Per-phase COLONY_STATE.json checkpointing. Only entomb creates backups. State-safety skill prescribes backups but they're not implemented in builds/continues/autopilot. Fix: cp CO
evidence: Oracle research: What would an expert change about this architecture to minimize user input while maximizing safety, verifiability, and autonomous execution capability?

instincts.action: Extended or modified bin module code
